2011-09-01.title: In Germany, Sex Workers Feed a Meter
2011-09-01.abstract: Bonn has hit upon the idea of a ticket machine that prints receipts for the privilege of streetwalking.

2011-09-01.title: Seeking Space, Well-to-Do Londoners Dig Deep
2011-09-01.abstract: Wealthy London homeowners who have run out of ways to expand have begun digging underground to put in pools, gyms and other additions.

2011-09-01.abstract: Denis Mamadou Cuspert, a former rapper, is accused of using inflammatory videos and fiery speeches to incite terrorism and violence against the West.

2011-09-01.abstract: WikiLeaks is at it again: news organizations are panning for nuggets, and diplomats are worrying about reprisals.

2011-09-01.title: Praising NATO, and France’s Place in It
2011-09-01.abstract: President Nicolas Sarkozy of France called NATO’s support of Libyan rebels “an indispensable tool,” and stressed the need for Europe to focus on its military capabilities.

2011-09-02.abstract: In India, workers are creating what will be the world’s largest biometric database, a collection of 1.2 billion identities.

2011-09-02.title: Greens Gain in Germany, and the World Takes Notice
2011-09-02.abstract: A string of victories and strong electoral showings are helping to redefine politics among voters in Germany and elsewhere who are increasingly losing faith in the more established parties.

2011-09-02.abstract: Hundreds of men freed from Abu Salim last week escaped Libya’s darkest corner, where for decades thousands of people disappeared, for years or forever.

2011-09-03.abstract: The decision by Turkey, a NATO member and an increasingly influential force in Middle Eastern politics, came against a backdrop of new Turkish frictions with Iran.

2011-09-03.title: In Russia, Harsh Remedy for Addiction Gains Favor
2011-09-03.abstract: Centers like City Without Drugs employ a detoxification and rehabilitation program that would be considered harsh by Western standards.

2011-09-03.abstract: Documents at an abandoned office in Tripoli show that Libya’s cooperation with the C.I.A. and its British equivalent, MI-6, was much more extensive than generally known.

2011-09-03.title: More Chinese Dissidents Appear to Disappear
2011-09-03.abstract: Experts say 2011 has seen a sharp and worrisome increase inside China of a security tactic that a United Nations international convention has sought to outlaw since 2010.

2011-09-05.abstract: Libyan rebels have imprisoned hundreds of migrants from sub-Saharan Africa, accusing them of being mercenaries for Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi.

2011-09-05.abstract: Libyan rebels have imprisoned hundreds of migrants from sub-Saharan Africa, accusing them of being mercenaries for Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi.

2011-09-06.abstract: Tehran, in its first counterproposal in two years, seeks to have mounting sanctions lifted by the West.

2011-09-06.abstract: Mr. Vann Nath was a painter and one of only a handful of survivors of the Khmer Rouge torture center Tuol Sleng.

2011-09-06.abstract: New details have emerged in the case of an F.B.I. translator who was sentenced last year to 20 months in prison for leaking classified information to a blogger.

2011-09-08.abstract: Investigators questioned three people in the state of Jammu and Kashmir over e-mails believed to have been sent by a militant group that claimed responsibility for the bombing.

2011-09-10.abstract: As the Shabab militant Islamist group has been pushed back, the central government has been too weak to fill the power vacuum.

2011-09-10.abstract: Libyan rebels began what they called full-scale attacks to subdue the loyalist holdouts of Bani Walid and Surt on Friday night.

2011-09-10.title: Violent Protest in Cairo
2011-09-10.abstract: A demonstration that brought tens of thousands to this city’s central Tahrir Square turned violent on Friday.

2011-09-11.title: Iraqi Cleric Tells Followers to Halt Attacks
2011-09-11.abstract: Violence against American troops would resume if they failed to leave Iraq by year’s end, Moktada al-Sadr said.

2011-09-11.abstract: A rebel push to take the loyalist holdout of Bani Walid stalled after encountering resistance from the defenders.

2011-09-11.abstract: Before 9/11 only the language of weapons worked in Afghanistan.

2011-09-12.abstract: The account offered by the agent, Ali H. Soufan, is the most detailed to date by an insider concerning the American investigations of Al Qaeda and the major attacks that the group carried out.

2011-09-12.abstract: The offer, discovered by a Canadian journalist in documents tossed into a Tripoli trash heap, flouted a United Nations embargo on weapons sales to the Qaddafi government.

2011-09-21.abstract: Disclosures from WikiLeaks indicated that Al Jazeera’s top news director, Wadah Khanfar, had modified the network’s coverage of the Iraq war in response to pressure from the United States.

2011-09-21.abstract: A government panel is expected to release a report on the high-speed train crash but survivors say they have already reached their own conclusions.

2011-09-21.abstract: To a growing number of critics, the government of Prime Minister Silvio Berlusconi has devolved into a royal court, in which everyone serves at the pleasure of the prince.

2011-09-22.abstract: Will this moment of unparalleled prestige for President Mahmoud Abbas, the Palestinian leader, produce concrete results or a new and more dangerous set of risks?

2011-09-22.abstract: Pope Benedict XVI will be greeted with demands for change on celibacy for priests, women’s role in the church, gay rights and aid for abuse victims.

2011-09-26.abstract: Qaddafi loyalists attacked the city of Ghadamis. Transitional government leaders said they discovered a mass grave.

2011-09-26.title: Libyans Say Qaddafi Loyalists Mounted Raid From Algeria
2011-09-26.abstract: Officials of the transitional government said supporters of Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi crossed the border to attack the town of Ghadamis.

2011-09-27.title: Japan: Aides Convicted in Campaign Finance Case
2011-09-27.abstract: A court on Monday convicted three former aides of Ichiro Ozawa, the embattled Japanese political kingmaker, of charges related to political campaign financing.

2011-09-27.title: China: Officers Suspended in Sex Slave Case
2011-09-27.abstract: Four police officers have been suspended because of negligence in the case of a former firefighter who raped and imprisoned six women, according to an article in China Daily.

2011-09-27.title: A Governing Coalition, Increasingly, of the Unwilling
2011-09-27.abstract: With unease in both parties, Britain’s first peacetime coalition government in nearly 70 years has come to resemble a leaky boat in heaving seas.

2011-09-27.title: Pakistanis Tied to 2007 Border Attack on Americans
2011-09-27.abstract: Details of the ambush suggest officials knew of Pakistan’s sometimes duplicitous role long before it was linked to an attack on the U.S. Embassy in Kabul.

2011-09-27.title: Fighters Enter Qaddafi Stronghold City as Toll Rises
2011-09-27.abstract: The latest strike into the coastal city of Surt follows a number of recent attacks that have been beaten back by Col. Muammar el-Qaddafi’s loyalists.

2011-09-28.title: In Pakistani Media, the U.S. Is a Target for Acrimony
2011-09-28.abstract: Although some voices have urged restraint, many prominent Pakistanis have speculated on the possibility of an American attack in the future.

2011-09-28.title: As Thousands Leave Libya, and Jobs, Niger Feels Impact
2011-09-28.abstract: Thousands of Nigeriens who have fled Libya are now destitute, hungry and dependent on a government that admits it cannot help them.

2011-09-28.title: As Scorn for Vote Grows, Protests Surge Around Globe
2011-09-28.abstract: Protesters around the world have something in common: wariness, even contempt, toward traditional politicians and the democratic political process they preside over.

2011-09-28.title: Appeal Trial Nears End for American Student
2011-09-28.abstract: Italian prosecutors called Amanda Knox, the American student convicted of killing her roommate, a femme fatale and “she-devil” while the defense said she was misunderstood.

2011-09-28.title: Saudi Arabia: 10 Lashes for Driving
2011-09-28.abstract: Two days after King Abdullah granted women the right to vote and run for public office, a court sentenced a woman to 10 lashes for violating religious rules prohibiting women from driving.

2011-09-29.title: U.S. Recalibrates Remarks About Pakistan
2011-09-29.abstract: Adm. Mike Mullen called the embassy attackers “a veritable arm” of Pakistan’s spy agency. An Obama spokesman would not go that far.

2011-09-29.title: Disagreements at U.N. Stall Sanctions on Syria
2011-09-29.abstract: Western nations rejected the wording of a Russian resolution, while Russia disparaged what it called a stealth attempt to oust the Syrian government.

2011-09-29.title: Mexico: Uncertain Ruling on Abortion
2011-09-29.abstract: After a closely watched debate, Mexico’s Supreme Court refused to throw out a state anti-abortion amendment on Wednesday.

2011-09-29.title: Iran: Production of New Missile Begins
2011-09-29.abstract: Iran announced the mass production of a new cruise missile on Wednesday.

2011-09-29.title: Yemen: Rebels Shoot Down Military Jet
2011-09-29.abstract: Rebel tribesmen in a mountainous region just north of Yemen’s capital, Sana, shot down a military aircraft on Wednesday, the Defense Ministry said.

2011-09-30.title: Optimism of Intellectuals Ebbs in Iraq
2011-09-30.abstract: A murder has led many who longed for Saddam Hussein’s overthrow to reconsider the aftermath of his departure.

2011-09-30.title: Housing Debate Unfolds in Shadow of a Living Wall
2011-09-30.abstract: A housing shortage in the area around Geneva has renewed a debate about Le Lignon, a residential behemoth with 2,780 apartments built four decades ago.

2011-09-30.title: Activists in Arab World Vie to Define Islamic State
2011-09-30.abstract: Many Islamic political activists say struggles between puritanical and liberal Islamists may now be more important than their battles against secularists.

2011-09-30.title: TimesCast | Post-Islamism and Elections
2011-09-30.abstract: September 29, 2011 — As senior U.S. officials meet leaders in Libya, Islamists in other liberated countries debate their evolving role in scheduled elections.

2011-09-30.title: Pakistani Politicians Reject Mullen’s Charges
2011-09-30.abstract: Top officials met in Islamabad amid speculation that the United States planned to move against Haqqani bases said to be in Pakistan.
